commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=217019ec134335ee65682bf8aaf8b35a654d11d0)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes shared Google message conversion so consecutive parallel tool results are accumulated into one function-response user turn before any deferred Gemini <3 image result turns.

PR surface: Source +13, Tests +78. Total +91 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: current main's last-content heuristic can split a parallel tool-result run when a Gemini <3 image turn is inserted between responses, and the PR adds a deterministic repro for that payload shape. I did not execute local tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor action is needed for the failing test-type lane and the missing real behavior proof; automation cannot supply their live Gemini environment proof.

target.title: fix(google): keep parallel Gemini tool responses in the turn after the model
target.body:
## Summary

On Gemini < 3 vision models (gemini-2.5-*), a single assistant turn with parallel tool calls where a non-last result returns an image corrupts the request so the next call returns 400 INVALID_ARGUMENT. Because the malformed turn is persisted in session history, every subsequent turn re-serializes it and re-400s, so the session is stuck until the user compacts or starts over.

Root cause is in `convertMessages` (`src/llm/providers/google-shared.ts`). Gemini's contract: the user turn immediately following a model turn with N functionCall parts must carry all N matching functionResponse parts, or the API returns 400 (confirmed against gemini-cli #16135 and litellm #26755). For Gemini < 3 (no multimodal functionResponse) an image-bearing tool result emits a separate "Tool result image:" user turn. The old merge heuristic only inspected `contents[contents.length - 1]`: when that separate image turn lands between two parallel function responses, the heuristic sees the image turn (not a functionResponse turn) and starts a fresh user turn for the second response. The turn right after the model then holds only 1 of 2 responses, so the count mismatches and Gemini 400s. It is order-dependent: if the image-bearing result is last it merges fine, which is why it slipped through.

Produced `contents` before the fix: `[user, model(fc1,fc2), user(fr1), user(image), user(fr2)]`, with fr2 stranded behind the image turn.

## Fix

Replace the fragile `contents[last]` heuristic with a run-scoped accumulator. All function responses for one model turn merge into the single user turn right after it; Gemini < 3 image turns are deferred and flushed at the end of the tool-result run so they trail the merged response turn. After the fix: `[user, model(fc1,fc2), user(fr1,fr2), user(image)]`, both responses in the immediately following turn.

Both Gemini surfaces, `google.ts` (Gemini API) and `google-vertex.ts` (Cloud Code Assist / Vertex), route through this single `convertMessages`, so the one change covers both. This is a latent bug in the Gemini-<3 separate-image-turn path (landed around 2026-05-31), not a fresh regression.

## Verification

Local, from repo root:

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
  src/llm/providers/google-shared.parallel-image-repro.test.ts \
  src/llm/providers/google-shared.convert.test.ts \
  src/llm/providers/google-shared.test.ts
```

Before (production file restored to origin/main, regression test kept):

```
 × keeps both parallel function responses in the user turn immediately after the model turn
   AssertionError: expected 1 to be 2
```

After (with fix):

```
 Test Files  3 passed (3)
      Tests  14 passed (14)
```

oxlint clean on both changed files.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Gemini < 3 parallel tool-call turn with a non-last image result split function responses across user turns, producing a response-count mismatch that 400s and, because history is persisted, keeps 400-ing on every later turn (stuck session).

Real environment tested: local repo checkout at current origin/main, Node 22.19, Vitest unit config; the conversion path is a pure function that builds the request payload, so no network call is involved.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/llm/providers/google-shared.parallel-image-repro.test.ts src/llm/providers/google-shared.convert.test.ts src/llm/providers/google-shared.test.ts`

Evidence after fix: 3 files passed, 14 tests passed (new repro test plus 11 convert and 2 google-shared regression tests). Reverting only the production file to origin/main while keeping the test reproduces the failure `expected 1 to be 2`, confirming the test fails without the fix and passes with it.

Observed result after fix: the user turn immediately after the model carries both function responses; the "Tool result image:" turn trails it; no function response is stranded in any later turn.

What was not tested: no live Gemini 2.5 API round-trip (would need gemini-2.5 credentials). Proof is through the deterministic `convertMessages` payload builder that produces the exact `contents` array sent to the API.
